CREATE TABLE publishers (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(100));
CREATE TABLE subscriptions (id INT PRIMARY KEY, publisher_id INT, status VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, status) VALUES (1, 1, 'ACTIVE');
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, status) VALUES (2, 1, 'ACTIVE');
INSERT INTO subscriptions (id, publisher_id, status) VALUES (3, 1, 'ACTIVE');
SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id = (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name = 'demo-srv');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id IN (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name LIKE 'demo-%');
SELECT p.name, COUNT(s.id) AS active_subscriptions FROM publishers p LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON p.id = s.publisher_id WHERE s.status = 'ACTIVE' GROUP BY p.name;
UPDATE subscriptions SET status = 'OFF' WHERE publisher_id = (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name = 'demo-srv');
SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE status IN (SELECT status FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id = 1);
SELECT * FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id = (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name = 'demo-srv');
DELETE FROM subscriptions WHERE publisher_id IN (SELECT id FROM publishers WHERE name = 'demo-srv');
